verses.text: 'Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul,' he cries, sinking in deep mire, weary with crying. He is overwhelmed by trouble. The drowning soul lifts its last strength to call on God.
'Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul,' he cries, sinking in deep mire, weary with crying. He is overwhelmed by trouble. The drowning soul lifts its last strength to call on God.
'Save me, O God; for the waters are come in unto my soul,' he cries, sinking in deep mire, weary with crying. He is overwhelmed by trouble. The drowning soul lifts its last strength to call on God.
Those who hate him 'without a cause' outnumber the hairs of his head. Christ took these words as His own. The sinless Saviour was hated for no fault, fulfilling this psalm.
Those who hate him 'without a cause' outnumber the hairs of his head. Christ took these words as His own. The sinless Saviour was hated for no fault, fulfilling this psalm.

He prays that the godly not be shamed through him, though he has become a stranger to his own brothers. Reproach has isolated him. The faithful sufferer cares more for others' faith than his own relief.

He prays that the godly not be shamed through him, though he has become a stranger to his own brothers. Reproach has isolated him. The faithful sufferer cares more for others' faith than his own relief.

He prays that the godly not be shamed through him, though he has become a stranger to his own brothers. Reproach has isolated him. The faithful sufferer cares more for others' faith than his own relief.
'The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up,' and the reproaches aimed at God fell on him. John and Paul both apply this to Christ. His devotion to God's honour drew on Himself the world's contempt.
'The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up,' and the reproaches aimed at God fell on him. John and Paul both apply this to Christ. His devotion to God's honour drew on Himself the world's contempt.
'The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up,' and the reproaches aimed at God fell on him. John and Paul both apply this to Christ. His devotion to God's honour drew on Himself the world's contempt.
'The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up,' and the reproaches aimed at God fell on him. John and Paul both apply this to Christ. His devotion to God's honour drew on Himself the world's contempt.
Yet he keeps praying in the acceptable time, asking to be drawn out of the mire and deep waters. He clings to God amid the flood. Even when sinking, faith keeps its grip on the God who saves.
Yet he keeps praying in the acceptable time, asking to be drawn out of the mire and deep waters. He clings to God amid the flood. Even when sinking, faith keeps its grip on the God who saves.
Yet he keeps praying in the acceptable time, asking to be drawn out of the mire and deep waters. He clings to God amid the flood. Even when sinking, faith keeps its grip on the God who saves.
He pleads God's good lovingkindness to turn to him and 'draw nigh unto my soul, and redeem it.' He begs God's nearness. The soul in extremity longs above all for God to come close.
He pleads God's good lovingkindness to turn to him and 'draw nigh unto my soul, and redeem it.' He begs God's nearness. The soul in extremity longs above all for God to come close.
He pleads God's good lovingkindness to turn to him and 'draw nigh unto my soul, and redeem it.' He begs God's nearness. The soul in extremity longs above all for God to come close.

God knows his reproach and shame, for 'they gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.' This was fulfilled at the cross. The Saviour drank the very bitterness this verse foretold.

God knows his reproach and shame, for 'they gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.' This was fulfilled at the cross. The Saviour drank the very bitterness this verse foretold.

God knows his reproach and shame, for 'they gave me also gall for my meat; and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.' This was fulfilled at the cross. The Saviour drank the very bitterness this verse foretold.
He calls for judgment on his persistent foes, that their table become a snare. Paul cites this of those who rejected Christ. The mercy spurned becomes at last a just and dreadful recompense.
He calls for judgment on his persistent foes, that their table become a snare. Paul cites this of those who rejected Christ. The mercy spurned becomes at last a just and dreadful recompense.
He calls for judgment on his persistent foes, that their table become a snare. Paul cites this of those who rejected Christ. The mercy spurned becomes at last a just and dreadful recompense.
He calls for judgment on his persistent foes, that their table become a snare. Paul cites this of those who rejected Christ. The mercy spurned becomes at last a just and dreadful recompense.

They persecute the one God has smitten and add to his pain, so let them be blotted from 'the book of the living.' Their cruelty seals their doom. To trample the afflicted of God is to forfeit a place among His own.

They persecute the one God has smitten and add to his pain, so let them be blotted from 'the book of the living.' Their cruelty seals their doom. To trample the afflicted of God is to forfeit a place among His own.

They persecute the one God has smitten and add to his pain, so let them be blotted from 'the book of the living.' Their cruelty seals their doom. To trample the afflicted of God is to forfeit a place among His own.

'But I am poor and sorrowful,' he says; God's salvation will lift him, and he will praise His name with a song, pleasing God more than sacrifice. Sorrow turns to praise. A thankful heart is the offering God prizes most.

'But I am poor and sorrowful,' he says; God's salvation will lift him, and he will praise His name with a song, pleasing God more than sacrifice. Sorrow turns to praise. A thankful heart is the offering God prizes most.

'But I am poor and sorrowful,' he says; God's salvation will lift him, and he will praise His name with a song, pleasing God more than sacrifice. Sorrow turns to praise. A thankful heart is the offering God prizes most.
The humble shall see it and be glad, for 'the LORD heareth the poor' and does not despise His prisoners. His mercy cheers the lowly. God's care for one sufferer is hope for all the afflicted.
The humble shall see it and be glad, for 'the LORD heareth the poor' and does not despise His prisoners. His mercy cheers the lowly. God's care for one sufferer is hope for all the afflicted.
Let heaven and earth praise Him, for God will save Zion and His servants' seed shall inherit it. The psalm ends in hope. Out of the deepest suffering springs the sure salvation of God's people.
Let heaven and earth praise Him, for God will save Zion and His servants' seed shall inherit it. The psalm ends in hope. Out of the deepest suffering springs the sure salvation of God's people.
